text.title: Substack-to-Product Funnel Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Substack-to-Product Funnel application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Substack Recommendations is the #1 organic growth lever. Target ≥10 active recommenders in 90 days; recommend ≥10 others.
rule.rationale: Substack data: recommendations drive 40-60% of new subscribers for active publications; this IS the channel.

rule.statement: Paid tier offers: full archives, weekly deep-dive, community thread, monthly call. Free preview of last 3-5 posts.
rule.rationale: Paid Substack tier needs to feel like a product; thin paywalls (just 'no ads') don't convert.

rule.statement: Cross-post to ≥1 other Substack/Beehiiv/Medium/LinkedIn weekly. Audience overlap is small enough to be additive.
rule.rationale: Single-channel distribution stalls at 1-2k subs; cross-posting unlocks parallel growth.

rule.statement: Cadence ≥weekly + same day same time. Substack algorithm + reader habit both reward consistency.
rule.rationale: Empirical: irregular schedule reduces open rate 30-50% + algorithm reach.

rule.statement: Track 60-day subscription cliff. Subscribers churning at 60-90 days = onboarding gap. Add: welcome series, top-5-posts, community invite.
rule.rationale: First-90-day cohort dominates churn distribution; explicit onboarding mitigates.
